other_observers: Nick Komar, Sol Miller, Sue Riffe, Andrew Spencer; Nick Komar and myself
description: It looked and acted like a goose. It was a little smaller than the Richardson's Cackling Goose, which the Brant was with. It had a short neck, and bulky.  It had a small bill. It had a black face, including cheek, and neck. One side of the neck had a very small white stripe. It also had very light sides, which contrasted sharply with the dark breast and neck. The back was gray with white edges to the coverts and dark primaries. It had a white undertail covert. I never saw the belly.Behaviors: When I saw it it was swimming along the far shore of the reservoir for about 15 minutes. It then had disappeared for some time and I refound it about 5 minutes before it went into a cove and I never saw it again.Call: didn't hear itPlumage: atlantic probable juvenile
